pattern.title: CVE-2002-2443: MIT Kerberos UDP Loop DoS
pattern.error_description: schpw.c in the kpasswd service in kadmind in MIT Kerberos 5 (aka krb5) before 1.11.3 does not properly validate UDP packets before sending responses, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (CPU and bandwidth consumption) via a forged packet that triggers a communication loop, as demonstrated by krb_pingpong.nasl, a related issue to CVE-1999-0103.
pattern.problematic_pattern: Loop without exit guard in UDP packet processing. Evidence: schpw.c does not properly validate UDP packets before sending responses, leading to communication loops.
pattern.solution: Add loop exit guards and request throttling, fail fast on invalid state, and create replay/fuzz tests for abusive sequences.

session_meta.user_message: Ingesting BigVul top 20 vulnerability data - CVE-2002-2443

issue.problem_phenomenon: UDP packet triggers infinite communication loop
issue.root_cause: Improper validation of UDP packets before response within schpw.c
issue.solution: Implement state transition guards and validation on incoming UDP packets

pattern.title: CVE-2006-3635: Linux Kernel ia64 RSE DoS
pattern.error_description: The ia64 subsystem in the Linux kernel before 2.6.26 allows local users to cause a denial of service (stack consumption and system crash) via a crafted application that leverages the mishandling of invalid Register Stack Engine (RSE) state.
pattern.problematic_pattern: Unchecked arithmetic or index usage may cause out-of-bounds access or stack consumption in RSE state handling.
pattern.solution: Add bounds checks before array/pointer operations, guard integer arithmetic, and include sanitizer-backed tests (ASAN/UBSAN).

session_meta.user_message: Ingesting BigVul top 20 vulnerability data - CVE-2006-3635

issue.problem_phenomenon: System crash via crafted application leveraging RSE state
issue.root_cause: Mishandling of invalid Register Stack Engine (RSE) state in ia64 subsystem
issue.solution: Implement rigorous validation for RSE state transitions
